% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input colours are given externally (input/3) – colour/1 is supplied.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Distinct colours that appear in the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------
has_color(V)           :- input(_,_,V).
distinct_count(N)      :- N = #count { V : has_color(V) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Smallest colour – decides which diagonal we use
% --------------------------------------------------------------
smallest_color(SC)     :- SC = #min { V : has_color(V) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Off‑diagonal occurrences of the smallest colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
off_diag(O)            :- O = #count { R,C : input(R,C,SC), R != C }.

% Helper predicates that test whether the count is 0 or >0
odd_zero    :- off_diag(O), O = 0.
odd_nonzero :- off_diag(O), O != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Choose diagonal orientation (relevant only when >1 colour)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
orientation_main :- distinct_count(N), N != 1, odd_zero.
orientation_anti :- distinct_count(N), N != 1, odd_nonzero.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Single‑colour vs. multiple‑colour case
% --------------------------------------------------------------
single_color   :- distinct_count(N), N = 1.
multiple_color :- distinct_count(N), N != 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  The result grid – fixed 3×3 (as in the training examples)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C)   :- R = 0..2, C = 0..2.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Cells forced to colour 5 (the diagonal)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
forced5_main(R,C)  :- orientation_main, R = C,    output_cell(R,C).
forced5_anti(R,C)  :- orientation_anti,  R + C = 2, output_cell(R,C).
forced5(R,C)       :- forced5_main(R,C).
forced5(R,C)       :- forced5_anti(R,C).
forced5(R,C)       :- single_color, R = 0, output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Cells forced to colour 0
% --------------------------------------------------------------
forced0(R,C)       :- single_color,  R != 0, output_cell(R,C).
forced0(R,C)       :- multiple_color,  not forced5(R,C), output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Exactly one colour per cell – allowed colour(s)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,V) :- forced5(R,C), V = 5.
allowed(R,C,V) :- forced0(R,C), V = 0.

% Choose the colour for each cell (col/1 facts come from the harness)
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  10.  A cell may not receive both colours
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,5), output(R,C,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  11.  The program must produce exactly ONE answer set.
%      The 1{…}1 rule together with the previous constraints
%      forces a unique answer set for every training example.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Which colours appear in the input?
% --------------------------------------------------------------
has_color(V) :- input(_,_,V).

distinct_count(Nc) :- Nc = #count { V : has_color(V) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Grid size  (square grid → rows = cols)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% make the dimensions known early and guarantee positivity
grid_safe :- max_row(Rmax), max_col(Cmax), Rmax = Cmax.

% extra rule to make Rmax and Cmax appear positively
dims :- max_row(Rmax), max_col(Cmax), Rmax = Cmax.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Smallest colour (the one that decides the diagonal)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
smallest_color(S) :- S = #min { V : has_color(V) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Corner information
% --------------------------------------------------------------
tl_corner(S)  :- input(0,0,S).        % top‑left
br_corner(S)  :- input(Rmax,Rmax,S).% bottom‑right
tr_corner(S)  :- input(0,Rmax,S).    % top‑right
bl_corner(S)  :- input(Rmax,0,S).    % bottom‑left

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. How many different colours are there ?
% --------------------------------------------------------------
single_colour   :- distinct_count(Nc), Nc = 1.
multiple_colour :- distinct_count(Nc), Nc != 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Which diagonal has to be filled with colour 5 ?
% --------------------------------------------------------------
orientation_main :- multiple_colour, smallest_color(S), tr_corner(S).
orientation_main :- multiple_colour, smallest_color(S), bl_corner(S).

orientation_anti :- multiple_colour, smallest_color(S), tl_corner(S).
orientation_anti :- multiple_colour, smallest_color(S), br_corner(S).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Cells that may appear in the output (the whole square)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..Rmax, C = 0..Rmax.

% positive occurrence for safety of R and C
cell(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C).

% explicit positive binder for R and C (helps with negations later)
pre(R,C) :- cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Cells forced to colour 5 (the diagonal)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
forced5_main(R,C)   :- orientation_main,   R = C,        output_cell(R,C).
forced5_anti(R,C)   :- orientation_anti,   R + C = Rmax, output_cell(R,C).
forced5_single(R,C) :- single_colour,      R = 0,         output_cell(R,C).

forced5(R,C) :- forced5_main(R,C).
forced5(R,C) :- forced5_anti(R,C).
forced5(R,C) :- forced5_single(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Cells forced to colour 0
% --------------------------------------------------------------
forced0_single(R,C) :- single_colour, R != 0, output_cell(R,C).
forced0_multi(R,C)  :- multiple_colour,
                       not forced5(R,C), cell(R,C), output_cell(R,C).

forced0(R,C) :- forced0_single(R,C).
forced0(R,C) :- forced0_multi(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Final output facts (exactly one colour per cell)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,5) :- forced5(R,C).
output(R,C,0) :- forced0(R,C).

% forbid two different colours on the same cell
:- output(R,C,5), output(R,C,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 11. The answer set is forced – no guessing needed.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% Distinct colours
has_color(V) :- input(_,_,V).
distinct_count(N) :- N = #count { V : has_color(V) }.

% Smallest colour
smallest_color(SC) :- SC = #min { V : has_color(V) }.

% Off‑diagonal count of smallest colour
off_diag(O) :- O = #count { R,C : input(R,C,SC), R != C }.

% Single vs multiple colour
single_color :- distinct_count(N), N = 1.
multiple_color :- distinct_count(N), N != 1.

% Diagonal orientation (used only when more than one colour)
orientation_main :- multiple_color, off_diag(O), O = 0.
orientation_anti :- multiple_color, off_diag(O), O != 0.

% Output cell domain (3x3)
output_cell(R,C) :- R = 0..2, C = 0..2.

% Cells that get colour 5
forced5(R,C) :- orientation_main, R = C, output_cell(R,C).
forced5(R,C) :- orientation_anti,  R + C = 2, output_cell(R,C).
forced5(R,C) :- single_color, R = 0, output_cell(R,C).

% Cells that get colour 0
forced0(R,C) :- single_color, R != 0, output_cell(R,C).
forced0(R,C) :- multiple_color, not forced5(R,C), output_cell(R,C).

% Allowed colours per cell
allowed(R,C,5) :- forced5(R,C).
allowed(R,C,0) :- forced0(R,C).

% Choose exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), allowed(R,C,V) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% No cell may receive both colours
:- output(R,C,5), output(R,C,0).

% colour/1 and input/3 facts are supplied by the harness
```
